title: One Nourishing Physical Act Today
subtitle: A small daily activity for when the body has been running on depletion
excerpt: A simple activity for choosing one physical act today that gives the body something rather than takes from it. For the weeks when the body has been in service mode and nothing has been given back.

practice.whenToUse: Use on a morning when the body has been in service mode for too long and you want to do one thing today that is for it rather than from it.

content.text: A daily activity for choosing one physical thing today that nourishes rather than depletes. Not a workout, not a to-do item. One act that gives the body something it needs.
This morning, choose one physical act that gives the body something rather than taking from it. One act. Specific. Timed or bounded. You do it today.
A twenty-minute walk in daylight, not as transport but as the thing itself.
A proper breakfast sitting down, not grabbed while doing something else.
Lying flat on the floor for ten minutes with nothing required of you.
A hot bath without a screen.
Stretching for five minutes before getting out of bed.

The body running on depletion is not fixed by large acts. A single week of daily small nourishing acts changes the pattern more reliably than one good weekend and then back to nothing. The small act matters because it is repeatable. The body registers the pattern, not the scale.
Choose one thing. Do it today. Tomorrow choose one thing again.
